name: Paraguay (Republic of Paraguay / República del Paraguay)
summary: Paraguay is a Southern Cone civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code 1987 (Código Civil, Law 1183 of 1985) supplemented by the Children and Adolescents Code 2001 (Law 1680 of 2001). Parental authority (patria potestad) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 70-95 and Children and Adolescents Code arts. 70-93. The Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Children and Adolescents Courts (Juzgados de la Niñez y Adolescencia). Psychology profession is regulated through the Sociedad Paraguaya de Psicología and Ministerio de Salud Pública licensing framework. Paraguay is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Children and Adolescents Code art. 3. Paraguay acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 May 1998. Paraguay is a bilingual Spanish-Guarani official-language state — distinctive within Latin America.

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code drawing on Argentine civil-law substantive heritage (Vélez Sarsfield Code). Arts. 70-95 govern patria potestad and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Children and Adolescents Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children and Adolescents Code codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Sociedad Paraguaya de Psicología
professional_regulators.role: Peak professional society for psychologists in Paraguay.

anonymisation_convention: Paraguayan family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Paraguay independence + Dr Francia 26-year-dictatorship framework
key_developments.description: Paraguay independence 14 May 1811 from Spain — substantively distinctive Latin American independence framework. Subsequent José Gaspar Rodríguez de Francia dictatorship 1814-1840 substantively distinctive 26-year-isolationist-dictatorship framework. Foundational pre-modern-codification framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Paraguayan War 1864-1870 + Triple Alliance War + demographic-catastrophe framework
key_developments.description: Paraguayan War (War of the Triple Alliance) 1864-1870 substantively reshaping demographic framework — estimated 60-90% of adult-male-population killed (most-distinctive demographic-catastrophe in Latin American history). Foundational post-war reconstruction framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Stroessner coup + 35-year-authoritarian framework
key_developments.description: Stroessner coup d'état 4 May 1954 — substantively distinctive Latin American 35-year-authoritarian framework 1954-1989. Substantive Cold-War-era political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Civil Code 1985 (Law 1183) + post-Stroessner-era-codification framework
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code enacted Law 1183 of 23 December 1985 drawing on Argentine civil-law substantive heritage (Vélez Sarsfield Code). Arts. 70-95 govern patria potestad and child custody. Substantively significant family-law-codification framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Rodríguez coup + democratic-transition + post-Stroessner framework
key_developments.description: Andrés Rodríguez coup d'état 2-3 February 1989 ending 35-year Stroessner era — substantively distinctive Latin American democratic-transition framework. Foundational democratic-trajectory framework affecting subsequent Constitution-1992 and family-law-modernisation.

key_developments.title: Paraguay ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Paraguay ratified the UNCRC on 25 September 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2001 Children and Adolescents Code substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Paraguay 1992 + post-democratic-transition framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Paraguay 1992 adopted 20 June 1992 substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing post-Stroessner-era democratic constitutional framework. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence including bilingual Spanish-Guarani official-language framework (art. 140).

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Paraguay acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 May 1998 — substantively significant Hague Latin American accession affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with Argentina, Brazil, and other Hague-party states.

key_developments.title: Children and Adolescents Code (Law 1680) + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Federal Children and Adolescents Code enacted Law 1680 of 8 November 2001 codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle (art. 3) aligned with UNCRC obligations. Arts. 70-93 govern parental authority. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Southern Cone framework.

key_developments.title: Peña presidency + Defensoría del Pueblo + Secretaría Nacional de la Niñez y la Adolescencia
key_developments.description: Santiago Peña (Colorado Party / ANR) inauguration 15 August 2023 — continuing Colorado Party governance framework. Defensoría del Pueblo and Secretaría Nacional de la Niñez y la Adolescencia (SNNA) institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Code 1680/2001 + Civil Code 1985 framework. Multiple Corte Suprema decisions developing superior-interest-of-the-child interpretation. Substantively significant Southern Cone institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Corte Suprema de Justicia — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Peña presidency
key_developments.description: Corte Suprema de Justicia continues to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code 1985 arts. 70-95 + Children and Adolescents Code 2001 art. 3 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Santiago Peña (Colorado Party) presidency since 15 August 2023. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Paraguay operates a Spanish-civil-law family-law framework drawing on Argentine Vélez Sarsfield Code heritage — places Paraguay in the Southern Cone civil-law cluster with Argentina, Uruguay, Brazil.
Bilingual Spanish-Guarani official-language framework is structurally distinctive within Latin America in the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 1998 places Paraguay in the Hague Latin American cluster.
editorial_notes: Paraguay jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Dr-Francia-to-Peña trajectory: 1811-Paraguay-independence-+-Dr-Francia + 1864-Paraguayan-War-+-Triple-Alliance + 1954-Stroessner-coup-+-35-year-authoritarian + 1985-Civil-Code-Law-1183 + 1989-Rodríguez-coup-+-democratic-transition + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1992-Constitution-of-Paraguay-+-post-democratic-transition + 1998-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2001-Children-and-Adolescents-Code-Law-1680 + 2024-Corte-Suprema-+-Peña-presidency.
Civil-law Southern Cone (Civil Code 1985 Vélez Sarsfield-derivative + Children and Adolescents Code 2001 + bilingual Spanish-Guarani + Hague Convention 1980 accession 1998).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code 1985 + Children and Adolescents Code 2001 art. 3 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Southern-Cone + civil-law + Vélez-Sarsfield-derivative + bilingual-Spanish-Guarani-indigenous-official-language-distinctive-Latin-America + Dr-Francia-26-year-isolationist-dictatorship-1814-1840-distinctive + Paraguayan-War-Triple-Alliance-1864-1870-60-90-percent-adult-male-population-killed-most-distinctive-demographic-catastrophe-Latin-American-history + Stroessner-35-year-authoritarian-1954-1989 + Constitution-1992-Spanish-Guarani-bilingual-art-140 + Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
